AMD.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: AMD is in one of the strongest uptrends in the watchlist. Daily EMA alignment is perfectly bullish (10 EMA 316.48 > 21 EMA 285.88 > 50 EMA 251.69), fanning wide. ADX 74.94 with +DI 55.37 vs -DI 10.10 confirms an exceptionally strong trend — directional bias is unambiguously bullish. Hourly EMAs are also aligned uptrend (ema_24 > ema_48 > ema_120), confirming multi-timeframe directional agreement. MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Momentum is strongly accelerating across all ROC timeframes — ROC 5d +16.1%, ROC 10d +27.4%, ROC 21d +74.26% — all positive and expanding in a parabolic pattern. MACD histogram +7.02 is bullish. However, RSI 14 is 83.06 and RSI 21 is 86.46 (deeply overbought), and the Z-score 50 is 2.60 (statistically stretched). NO CLEAN ENTRY — NEUTRAL: Price at $354.49 is 12% above the 10 EMA ($316.48) and 24% above the 21 EMA ($285.88). Strategy rules require entry on a pullback to the 10 or 21 EMA — no such pullback exists. Entering here chases a parabolic move. Wiki memory confirms the prior session noted the same overextension ($200 to $353 parabolic, waiting for pullback to $308-$315 or breakout above $352.99). Price broke above $352.99 with volume today, but RSI is at extremes. The lessons file warns of an identical pattern failure: swing_trend_momentum on NVDA with RSI 81-87 produced a stop-out when price breached the stop zone without a bullish confirmation candle. Hourly ADX at 15.89 (weak) further warns that hourly momentum is fragmenting even as the daily trend is powerful — a divergence signaling potential exhaustion. Earnings on May 5 introduces binary event risk, violating prudent risk management for a trend entry. Wait for a pullback to the 10 EMA (~$316-$320) with ADX still above 25 and ROC re-accelerating before committing capital.
